title: Enums 🗂️
description: Define a type by enumerating its possible variants.

slides.title: The Power of Choice 🛣️
slides.content:
Structs group data together (`width` AND `height`).
**Enums** (enumerations) give you a choice: A value can be *this* OR *that*.

```rust
enum IpAddrKind {
    V4,
    V6,
}
```
An IP address can be V4 *or* V6, but never both at the same time.

slides.title: Using Enums 🗂️

quizData.question: What is the core difference between a Struct and an Enum?

options.text: Structs hold data; Enums only hold numbers.
A Struct requires ALL its fields to exist at once. An Enum instance represents exactly ONE of its defined variants at a time.

quizData.explanation: Structs combine things (A and B). Enums categorize choices (A or B).

slides.title: Data Within Variants 🎁
No Limits 🚀
slides.content:
Each enum variant can have wildly different types and amounts of associated data. You can even stick completely different Structs inside an Enum's variants!

This makes Rust's type system incredibly expressive.

quizData.question: In Rust, an Enum variant is strictly limited to only being a basic name (like `V4`); it cannot directly hold associated string or integer data within itself.
quizData.explanation: Unlike C or Java, Rust's Enums are essentially advanced unions. They can natively hold huge amounts of completely diverse data attached directly to the varying choices.
